reuters.com.name: Reuters
reuters.com.description: Highly objective, fact-based global news agency.

apnews.com.name: Associated Press
apnews.com.description: Global independent news organization, the gold standard for objective reporting.

bbc.com.name: BBC News
bbc.com.description: British public service broadcaster known for high-quality, international reporting.

bbc.co.uk.name: BBC News
bbc.co.uk.description: British public service broadcaster known for high-quality, international reporting.

bloomberg.com.name: Bloomberg
bloomberg.com.description: Highly reliable business, financial, and global news publisher.

npr.org.name: NPR
npr.org.description: American public radio network with strong investigative journalism and facts.

nytimes.com.name: The New York Times
nytimes.com.description: Highly reputable national newspaper, though occasionally carries an editorial bias.

washingtonpost.com.name: The Washington Post
washingtonpost.com.description: Highly reputable national newspaper known for thorough investigative reporting.

theguardian.com.name: The Guardian
theguardian.com.description: Reputable UK newspaper with left-leaning editorial stance, high factual standards.

wsj.com.name: Wall Street Journal
wsj.com.description: Reputable financial newspaper with conservative-leaning editorial board, high factual standards.

dw.com.name: Deutsche Welle
dw.com.description: German public broadcaster with reliable, objective international coverage.

afp.com.name: Agence France-Presse
afp.com.description: Leading global news agency committed to accurate, objective coverage.

axios.com.name: Axios
axios.com.description: Known for concise, objective reporting and analysis.

c-span.org.name: C-SPAN
c-span.org.description: Direct coverage of government proceedings. Absolute neutral factuality.

pbs.org.name: PBS NewsHour
pbs.org.description: Public broadcasting news program known for balanced, in-depth reports.

snopes.com.name: Snopes
snopes.com.description: Leading fact-checking website verifying urban legends and news rumors.

politifact.com.name: PolitiFact
politifact.com.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning fact-checking website focusing on political claims.

factcheck.org.name: FactCheck.org
factcheck.org.description: Non-partisan, non-profit consumer advocate monitoring political statements.

infowars.com.name: InfoWars
infowars.com.description: Far-right conspiracy website known for spreading false information and hoaxes.

naturalnews.com.name: Natural News
naturalnews.com.description: Conspiracy, anti-science, and alternative medicine website promoting false claims.

beforeitsnews.com.name: Before It's News
beforeitsnews.com.description: Unmoderated user-submitted news platform known for extreme conspiracy theories.

thegatewaypundit.com.name: The Gateway Pundit
thegatewaypundit.com.description: Far-right fake news website frequently publishing debunked stories and hoaxes.

wnd.com.name: WorldNetDaily
wnd.com.description: Conservative conspiracy theory and fringe news website.

zerohedge.com.name: ZeroHedge
zerohedge.com.description: Financial blog and news aggregator with a history of promoting conspiracy theories.

activistpost.com.name: Activist Post
activistpost.com.description: Alternative news blog promoting conspiracy theories and anti-government narratives.

theonion.com.name: The Onion
theonion.com.description: Satirical digital media company. Renders false news purely for entertainment and parody.

babylonbee.com.name: The Babylon Bee
babylonbee.com.description: Christian satirical news site targeting politics and current events.

clickhole.com.name: ClickHole
clickhole.com.description: Satirical website parodying clickbait media and internet culture.

dailymail.co.uk.name: Daily Mail
dailymail.co.uk.description: UK tabloid known for sensationalism, clickbait headlines, and factual inaccuracies.

dailymail.com.name: Daily Mail
dailymail.com.description: UK tabloid known for sensationalism, clickbait headlines, and factual inaccuracies.

nationalenquirer.com.name: National Enquirer
nationalenquirer.com.description: Supermarket tabloid carrying highly sensationalized stories and gossip.

weeklyworldnews.com.name: Weekly World News
weeklyworldnews.com.description: Fictional supermarket tabloid carrying bizarre paranormal claims (originally print, now online).

buzzfeed.com.name: Buzzfeed
buzzfeed.com.description: Internet media site known for viral lists, quizzes, and sensationalized headlines, distinct from BuzzFeed News.

tmz.com.name: TMZ
tmz.com.description: Celebrity gossip and entertainment website with clickbait formatting but often accurate scoop info.

perezhilton.com.name: Perez Hilton
perezhilton.com.description: Celebrity gossip blog utilizing sensationalism, tabloid styling, and personal opinions.
